title: After Action Report: NiceGUI 3.x Compatibility & SaaS UI Layout

rationale.item: The Viperball Sandbox app was rendering a blank white page with no visible errors. Browser showed repeated 'implicit handshake failed' messages and websocket connection would open then immediately close in a loop.

actions.description: nicegui_app/state.py: app.storage.tab → app.storage.user (cookie-based storage works without async client connection)
nicegui_app/app.py: async def index(client) → def index(), removed await client.connected()
nicegui_app/pages/play.py: added render_play_section_sync() sync entry point for initial page build

risk_posture: app.storage.user persists across all tabs (not per-tab like before). For a single-user sandbox tool, this is acceptable.
The initial page render is fully synchronous. Active session data loads asynchronously after render via a short timer, so there's a brief 'Loading...' flash for returning sessions.
residual_risks: app.storage.user persists across all tabs — competing sessions in different tabs may interfere
section_headers_original: After Action Report: NiceGUI 3.x Compatibility & SaaS UI Layout
unmapped_content: Root Cause Analysis: (1) app.storage.tab requires await client.connected() before it can be accessed; (2) await client.connected() in NiceGUI 3.x caused a deadlock when combined with await run.io_bound() API calls during page build because NiceGUI and FastAPI share the same uvicorn event loop; (3) catch-22: storage API needed async wait, async wait broke websocket lifecycle. Debugging Process: 8-step isolation process from checking server logs to explicit error message 'app.storage.tab can only be used with a client connection'. UI Outcome: SaaS-style horizontal navigation bar; no hidden menus/sidebar; session status and End button in header; mobile-friendly. NiceGUI Storage Reference table: client/tab/user/browser/general — scope, requires connected(), persists on reload, persists across tabs. Lessons: 1) blank NiceGUI page → check browser console for 'implicit handshake failed'; 2) avoid await during page build in shared event loop; 3) verify installed vs pinned package version; 4) app.storage.user is safest default.
parser_notes: Date: February 23, 2026 — confirmed in document header. Very short document — mostly root cause analysis and solution. No validation claims, no non_actions, no explicit scope statement.
